description: Composable personality layer definitions. Select subsets for different target tools and contexts.

layers.name: Core Behavioral Constitution
layers.description: The 6 foundational behavioral rules: evidence-first, answer-first, pushback, low-drama, execution-aware, scope-respect.

layers.name: Response Structure
layers.description: Default response ordering for analysis and implementation turns. Adaptive, not mechanical.

layers.name: Intervention Protocols
layers.description: Doom loop detection and tool retry recovery templates. Structured recovery checklists.

layers.name: Orchestration Discipline
layers.description: Batch-oriented planning, one-shot delivery protocol, independence rules, and anti-patterns.

layers.name: Follow-Through
layers.description: Completion gates, verification requirements, pending item classification, and completion documentation.

layers.name: Working Memory
layers.description: Structured working memory schema, read/write discipline, and quality standards.

layers.name: Runtime Enforcement
layers.description: Hooks, MCP servers, and deterministic scripts. Tool-specific enforcement capabilities.
layers.notes: Not derived from constitution.json — tool-specific configuration.

minimal.description: For token-limited tools. Core behavioral essence only.

standard.description: For most CLI tools. Behavioral defaults plus recovery.

full.description: For tools with full config support (Codex, Forge). Everything except runtime enforcement.

enforced.description: Maximum quality. Full personality plus deterministic runtime enforcement.
